message.content:
Ausgangslage

Airlock SaaS ist ein Managed Service für Airlock IAM in der Cloud. Kunden provisionieren, konfigurieren und verwalten ihre Tenant IAM Instanzen über die Airlock SaaS Plattform. Im Standardfall erfolgt die Konfiguration über ein ZIP File, welches Administratoren hochladen und aktivieren können. Zusätzlich gibt es einen Getting Started Wizard, der den Einstieg in die Plattform deutlich vereinfacht.

Der Getting Started Wizard ist ein stark vereinfachter Config Editor innerhalb von Airlock SaaS. Damit können Kunden schnell und einfach eine funktionsfähige Konfiguration für ihr Tenant IAM erstellen. Neben einem minimalen Set von ausgewählten Konfigurationsoptionen, bietet der Getting Started Wizard zentrale Interaktions- und Informationselemente zur Verwaltung des Tenant IAMs. Nachdem die gewünschte Konfiguration erstellt wurde, kann sie direkt im Wizard aktiviert werden. Während der Aktivierung wird der Fortschritt des Deployments angezeigt und anschliessend wird der Status des Tenant IAMs dargestellt. Dieser Status ist dynamisch und erkennt auch, wenn ein laufendes Tenant IAM nicht mehr erreichbar ist. Zusätzlich stellt der Wizard Links zur Adminapp und Loginapp des Tenant IAM bereit.

In der Übersicht fehlen jedoch wesentliche Informationen: Aktuell ist nicht ersichtlich, welche Konfiguration im laufenden Tenant IAM aktiv ist. Werden im Wizard Änderungen vorgenommen, nachdem bereits eine andere Konfiguration aktiviert wurde, wird dieser Unterschied nicht angezeigt. Damit ist nicht nachvollziehbar, ob eine Änderung bereits aktiviert wurde oder ob die im Wizard sichtbare Konfiguration vom tatsächlich laufenden Stand abweicht.

Die Herausforderung innerhalb dieses Projekts besteht darin, eine eindeutige und jederzeit aktuelle Zuordnung zwischen einer Konfiguration im Getting Started Wizard und der laufender Konfiguration herzustellen und im UI nutzerfreundlich und klar darzustellen, damit Änderungen gezielt überprüft und aktiviert werden können.


Detaillierte Aufgabenstellung

Motivation

Ein Airlock IAM wird file-basiert konfiguriert. Es gibt mehrere Dateien, welche vom IAM geladen werden, wodurch Features, Inhalte und das Design definiert werden. In Airlock SaaS wird dieses Set von Files als ZIP File gehandhabt. Im Standardfall, unabhängig vom Getting Started Wizard, können Administratoren ein solches ZIP File hochladen und aktivieren und damit ihr Tenant IAM konfigurieren.

Im Getting Started Wizard existiert ein Transformer Modul, welches die Wizard Konfiguration in ein ZIP File transformiert. Bei einer Aktivierung wird dieses ZIP File erzeugt, persistiert und anschliessend in allen Folgeprozessen, insbesondere im Deployment, als Konfigurationsartefakt verwendet. Dieses Vorgehen wurde bewusst gewählt, da der ZIP Case der Standardfall der Plattform ist und die Aktivierung damit konsistent über denselben Mechanismus erfolgt.

Daraus resultiert für den Getting Started Wizard jedoch eine zentrale Schwierigkeit: Nach der ZIP Transformation ist die ursprüngliche Wizard Konfiguration nicht mehr rekonstruierbar und es sind keine Informationen zum Inhalt der laufenden Konfiguration im Getting Started Wizard verfügbar. Damit kann der Wizard nicht bestimmen, ob die angezeigte Konfiguration dem aktiven Stand entspricht oder davon abweicht.

Der Kandidat soll diese Schwierigkeit lösen und konkret die folgende Funktionen ermöglichen:

Veränderungen können erkannt werden: Im Getting Started Wizard ist ersichtlich, ob sich die Wizard Konfiguration gegenüber der laufenden Konfiguration verändert hat.

Darüber hinaus soll der Kandidat analysieren, wie Veränderungen detailliert ermittelt werden könnten um nicht nur festzustellen, ob Veränderungen vorliegen, sondern auch was sich im Detail verändert hat. Dies ist jedoch keine Anforderung für den Wizard und soll daher lediglich für die Analyse berücksichtigt werden.

Analyse

Der Kandidat analysiert, wann und wie die Wizard Konfiguration gespeichert werden soll und wie sie neu im Aktivierungs- und Deploymentprozess gehandhabt werden muss. Hierzu sind mehrere Lösungsvarianten auszuarbeiten, zu vergleichen und fundiert zu begründen. Das Ergebnis ist eine empfohlene Zielarchitektur inklusive Datenmodell, Interaktionsarchitektur und Integrationskonzept.

Die Analyse muss mindestens folgende Aspekte abdecken:

Datenhaltung: Speicherort, Lebenszyklus, Retention und Cleanup
Diff-Berechnung: Strategie, Granularität, Determinismus und Vergleichbarkeit
Performance: Auswirkungen auf Speicherbedarf; Skalierung über Tenants und Aktivierungsfrequenz
Integration: Integration in bestehende Aktivierungs- und Deploymentpipeline
Auditierbarkeit: Nachvollziehbarkeit, wer wann welche Konfiguration gespeichert und aktiviert hat
Datenmigration: Notwendigkeit und Vorgehen (falls notwendig)
Implementation

Auf Basis der gewählten Variante soll die Lösung implementiert werden. Der Mindestumfang beinhaltet:

Die Datenhaltung wurde angepasst, dass eine Veränderung in der Wizard Konfiguration ermittelt werden kann
Die resultierenden Informationen sind im Wizard UI konsistent und fokussiert dargestellt. Nutzer sehen und verstehen, ob sich die Wizard Konfiguration von der laufenden Konfiguration unterscheidet

Use a subagents team to exmplore the code base then use another subagents team to explore and reason through variouse options we would have to implement this feature.

The user wants me to explore the codebase to understand the Getting Started Wizard and the configuration/deployment pipeline, then reason through various implementation options for detecting configuration changes between the wizard state and the active deployment.

Let me break this into two phases:
1. First, explore the codebase with a team of subagents to understand the current architecture
2. Then, use another team to reason through implementation options

Let me start with Phase 1 - exploring the codebase. I'll launch multiple exploration agents in parallel to cover different aspects.

<result>Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings.

## Frontend Getting Started Wizard UI - Detailed Findings

I've thoroughly explored the frontend codebase and identified the complete Getting Started Wizard implementation. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. **Main Wizard Page Structure**

**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/pages/configuration/tenants/[tenantId]/getting-started/GettingStarted.page.tsx`

- **Main Component:** `GettingStartedPage`
- **Key Features:**
  - Access control via `isWizardEnabled` flag - redirects to regular config if disabled
  - Uses `useRequiredTenantId` hook to get tenant context
  - Displays three main sections:
    1. Recommended workflow info zone (3 tips + contact link)
    2. **Configuration** section with `<ConfigurationActionGroup />`
    3. **Operation** section with `<OperationActionGroup />`
  - Uses React Router for navigation with `PublicPathBuilder`

### 2. **Wizard UI Components - Steps/Pages**

The wizard uses an **Action Group pattern** instead of traditional wizard steps. There are two main action group components:

#### **Configuration ActionGroup** 
**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/ConfigurationActionGroup.tsx`

Four numbered configuration action items:
1. **Application** - "Where do users land after login?" (modal: `ApplicationSettingsModal`)
2. **Authentication flow** - "How will your users authenticate?" (modal: `AuthenticationSettingsModal`)
3. **User onboarding** - "Who will create user accounts?" (modal: `UserOnboardingModal`)
4. **User experience** - "What will the UI look like?" (modal: `UserExperienceSettingsModal`)

Each item shows:
- Icon (numbered 1-4)
- Title and subtitle
- Current selection preview (actionPreview)
- Click handler to open corresponding modal

#### **Operation ActionGroup**
**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/OperationActionGroup.tsx`

Three action items:
1. **Deployment** - `<DeploymentActionGroupItem />` - Status and activation button
2. **Login form** - "Open the login form to test logging in" - Direct link to `deployment.loginUrl`
3. **User management** - Create test users and manage users - Link to adminapp

Also shows error panel if activation failed.

### 3. **Deployment Status and Progress Tracking**

**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/DeploymentActionGroupItem.tsx`

**Deployment Status States:**
- `Active` - Green rocket launch icon, "Your tenant is ready"
- `Inactive` - Gray rocket, "Activate tenant to enable login"
- `Pending` - Blue pending icon, "Activation in progress.. This may take a few minutes"
- `Unavailable` - Yellow pending icon, "Tenant cannot currently be reached"

**Key Logic:**
```typescript
function getActivationState(): ActivationState {
  if (isActivating) return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Pending;
  if (polledMostRecentActivation === null) return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Inactive;
  if (isDeploymentAvailable(deployment)) return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Active;
  // Failed states...
  return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Unavailable;
}
```

- Uses polling hooks to track state changes
- Shows optimistic UI updates while submitting (via `isSubmitting` state)
- Displays success/error notifications on completion

### 4. **Links to Adminapp and Loginapp**

**Adminapp (User Management):**
```typescript
const adminappUrl = `${import.meta.env.VITE_BASE_PATH}${new PublicPathBuilder().tenantIAMRedirect().build({ tenantId })}`;
```
- Shown in User Management action item
- Enabled only when deployment is available
- Disabled if mTLS is configured for adminapp

**Loginapp (Login Form):**
```typescript
link={{ href: deployment.loginUrl, label: 'Open login form' }}
disabled={!canLoginappBeOpened(deployment)}
```
- Enabled only when login URL exists and deployment is available

### 5. **Change Tracking and Dirty State**

**Findings:**
- **No explicit dirty state tracking** at the action group level
- Modal forms use **React Hook Form's `isDirty`** internally via `shouldDirty: true` in setValue calls
- **Example from UserExperienceSettingsModal:**
```typescript
setValue(name, color.hex, {
  shouldDirty: true,
});
```

**Pattern:**
- Each modal opens independently
- Form changes are submitted on modal close
- Parent components don't track pending changes across modals
- No global "dirty" indicator on the wizard itself

### 6. **API Calls and Data Flow**

**Data Fetching Hooks:**

1. **useSaasConfig** - Gets full wizard config
```typescript
useSuspenseQuery({
  queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'saas-config'],
  queryFn: () => saasConfigApi.getSaasConfig(tenantId),
})
```

2. **usePolledTenantDeployment** - Polls deployment status
```typescript
useQueryWithPolling({
  queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'deployment'],
  queryFn: () => tenantsApi.findTenantDeployment(tenantId),
})
```

3. **usePolledMostRecentConfigActivation** - Polls latest activation
```typescript
const { data: configActivations } = useConfigActivations(tenantId);
const mostRecentActivation = configActivations[0];
const { data: polledActivation } = useNullablePolledConfigActivation(tenantId, mostRecentActivation);
```

**Update Mutations Pattern:**

Example from `useUpdateAuthentication`:
```typescript
useMutation({
  mutationFn: (authentication: Authentication) =>
    authenticationApi.updateAuthentication(tenantId, authentication),
  onSuccess: async (authentication: Authentication) => {
    // Invalidate and refetch full saas-config
    await queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'saas-config'],
      refetchType: 'all',
      exact: true,
    });
    // Update specific sub-query cache
    queryClient.setQueryData(['tenants', tenantId, 'saas-config', 'authentication'], authentication);
    // Show success notification
    setNotification({ level: 'success', message: '...' });
  },
})
```

**Activation Flow:**

File: `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/use-activate-saas-config/useActivateSaasConfig.ts`

```typescript
useMutation({
  mutationFn: (tenantId: string) => saasConfigApi.activateSaasConfig(tenantId),
  onSuccess: async (configActivation: ConfigActivation, tenantId: string) => {
    // Invalidate and refetch config-activations
    await queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'config-activations'],
      refetchType: 'all',
    });
  },
})
```

### 7. **Configuration Domain Models**

**Files:**
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/domain/saasConfig.ts` - Full wizard config type
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/domain/configActivation.ts` - Activation tracking
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/domain/tenantDeployment.ts` - Deployment state

**ConfigActivation Types:**
```typescript
type ConfigActivation = {
  status: ConfigActivationStatus;
  activationDate: Date;
  statusUpdatedDate?: Date;
  config: Omit<Config, 'id'>;
}

enum ConfigActivationStatus {
  Pending = 'Pending',
  Progressing = 'Progressing',
  Success = 'Success',
  Failed = 'Failed',
  RejectedInvalidConfig = 'RejectedInvalidConfig',
  RejectedUnsupportedVersion = 'RejectedUnsupportedVersion',
}

function isConfigActivationOngoing(configActivation): boolean {
  return status === Pending || status === Progressing;
}
```

**TenantDeployment Helper Functions:**
```typescript
isDeploymentAvailable(deployment) // state === RUNNING && health === HEALTHY
isDeploymentRunning(deployment)
isDeploymentHealthy(deployment)
hasLoginUrl(deployment)
hasConfig(deployment)
hasDeployment(deployment)
canLoginappBeOpened(deployment)
hasAdminappMtlsEnabled(deployment)
```

### 8. **Modal Components for Configuration**

Each modal follows the same pattern:
- Fetches current config data with `useSuspenseQuery`
- Uses React Hook Form for form state
- Updates via mutation hooks
- Closes on successful save

**Modals Located At:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/modals/`

Key modals:
- `ApplicationSettingsModal` - Portal or OIDC app config
- `AuthenticationSettingsModal` - Password, passkey, or OTP flows + email translations
- `UserOnboardingModal` - Manual creation or self-registration with allowed domains
- `UserExperienceSettingsModal` - Branding (colors, logo), translations, preview
- `CreateTestUserModal` - Create temporary test user in deployment

### 9. **Wizard Enable/Disable Toggle**

**Files:**
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/tenants/base/WizardDeActivationCard.tsx`
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/tenants/base/WizardDeActivationModal.tsx`

**Functionality:**
- Toggle wizard on/off via `isWizardEnabled` tenant property
- Uses `useUpdateTenant` mutation
- Card shows different label based on current state ("Unlock" vs "Revert")
- Invalidates `['isWizardEnabled', tenantId]` query cache on success

### 10. **No Configuration Comparison**

**Finding:** There is **NO existing mechanism for comparing configurations**. The wizard:
- Only shows current state preview in action items
- Does not store draft/previous versions for comparison
- Does not show "what changed" indicators
- All changes are written directly to the backend

### 11. **API Wiring**

**Main API File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/api/saas-config/saasConfig.api.ts`

```typescript
class SaasConfigApi {
  getSaasConfig(tenantId: string): Promise<SaasConfig>
  activateSaasConfig(tenantId: string): Promise<ConfigActivation>
  getConfigZip(tenantId: string): Promise<{ blob: Blob; filename: string }>
}
```

Sub-APIs for each configuration section:
- `/api/saas-config/applications/applications.api.ts`
- `/api/saas-config/authentication/authentication.api.ts`
- `/api/saas-config/ui-customization/uiCustomizationApi.ts`
- `/api/saas-config/user-onboarding/userOnboarding.api.ts`

---

## Summary

The Getting Started Wizard is implemented as a **guided configuration UI with action-based steps** rather than traditional wizard steps. It uses:

- **React Query** for polling deployment/activation status
- **React Hook Form** for modal forms with no cross-modal state
- **TanStack Query mutations** for updates with automatic cache invalidation
- **Polling hooks** to track long-running activation processes
- **Status badges and icons** to show deployment readiness
- **Direct links** to adminapp and loginapp for testing

The wizard currently **lacks configuration change tracking and comparison** - all changes are immediate backend updates.</result>

## Frontend Getting Started Wizard UI - Detailed Findings

I've thoroughly explored the frontend codebase and identified the complete Getting Started Wizard implementation. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. **Main Wizard Page Structure**

**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/pages/configuration/tenants/[tenantId]/getting-started/GettingStarted.page.tsx`

- **Main Component:** `GettingStartedPage`
- **Key Features:**
  - Access control via `isWizardEnabled` flag - redirects to regular config if disabled
  - Uses `useRequiredTenantId` hook to get tenant context
  - Displays three main sections:
    1. Recommended workflow info zone (3 tips + contact link)
    2. **Configuration** section with `<ConfigurationActionGroup />`
    3. **Operation** section with `<OperationActionGroup />`
  - Uses React Router for navigation with `PublicPathBuilder`

### 2. **Wizard UI Components - Steps/Pages**

The wizard uses an **Action Group pattern** instead of traditional wizard steps. There are two main action group components:

#### **Configuration ActionGroup** 
**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/ConfigurationActionGroup.tsx`

Four numbered configuration action items:
1. **Application** - "Where do users land after login?" (modal: `ApplicationSettingsModal`)
2. **Authentication flow** - "How will your users authenticate?" (modal: `AuthenticationSettingsModal`)
3. **User onboarding** - "Who will create user accounts?" (modal: `UserOnboardingModal`)
4. **User experience** - "What will the UI look like?" (modal: `UserExperienceSettingsModal`)

Each item shows:
- Icon (numbered 1-4)
- Title and subtitle
- Current selection preview (actionPreview)
- Click handler to open corresponding modal

#### **Operation ActionGroup**
**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/OperationActionGroup.tsx`

Three action items:
1. **Deployment** - `<DeploymentActionGroupItem />` - Status and activation button
2. **Login form** - "Open the login form to test logging in" - Direct link to `deployment.loginUrl`
3. **User management** - Create test users and manage users - Link to adminapp

Also shows error panel if activation failed.

### 3. **Deployment Status and Progress Tracking**

**File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/DeploymentActionGroupItem.tsx`

**Deployment Status States:**
- `Active` - Green rocket launch icon, "Your tenant is ready"
- `Inactive` - Gray rocket, "Activate tenant to enable login"
- `Pending` - Blue pending icon, "Activation in progress.. This may take a few minutes"
- `Unavailable` - Yellow pending icon, "Tenant cannot currently be reached"

**Key Logic:**
```typescript
function getActivationState(): ActivationState {
  if (isActivating) return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Pending;
  if (polledMostRecentActivation === null) return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Inactive;
  if (isDeploymentAvailable(deployment)) return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Active;
  // Failed states...
  return DEPLOYMENT_STATES.Unavailable;
}
```

- Uses polling hooks to track state changes
- Shows optimistic UI updates while submitting (via `isSubmitting` state)
- Displays success/error notifications on completion

### 4. **Links to Adminapp and Loginapp**

**Adminapp (User Management):**
```typescript
const adminappUrl = `${import.meta.env.VITE_BASE_PATH}${new PublicPathBuilder().tenantIAMRedirect().build({ tenantId })}`;
```
- Shown in User Management action item
- Enabled only when deployment is available
- Disabled if mTLS is configured for adminapp

**Loginapp (Login Form):**
```typescript
link={{ href: deployment.loginUrl, label: 'Open login form' }}
disabled={!canLoginappBeOpened(deployment)}
```
- Enabled only when login URL exists and deployment is available

### 5. **Change Tracking and Dirty State**

**Findings:**
- **No explicit dirty state tracking** at the action group level
- Modal forms use **React Hook Form's `isDirty`** internally via `shouldDirty: true` in setValue calls
- **Example from UserExperienceSettingsModal:**
```typescript
setValue(name, color.hex, {
  shouldDirty: true,
});
```

**Pattern:**
- Each modal opens independently
- Form changes are submitted on modal close
- Parent components don't track pending changes across modals
- No global "dirty" indicator on the wizard itself

### 6. **API Calls and Data Flow**

**Data Fetching Hooks:**

1. **useSaasConfig** - Gets full wizard config
```typescript
useSuspenseQuery({
  queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'saas-config'],
  queryFn: () => saasConfigApi.getSaasConfig(tenantId),
})
```

2. **usePolledTenantDeployment** - Polls deployment status
```typescript
useQueryWithPolling({
  queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'deployment'],
  queryFn: () => tenantsApi.findTenantDeployment(tenantId),
})
```

3. **usePolledMostRecentConfigActivation** - Polls latest activation
```typescript
const { data: configActivations } = useConfigActivations(tenantId);
const mostRecentActivation = configActivations[0];
const { data: polledActivation } = useNullablePolledConfigActivation(tenantId, mostRecentActivation);
```

**Update Mutations Pattern:**

Example from `useUpdateAuthentication`:
```typescript
useMutation({
  mutationFn: (authentication: Authentication) =>
    authenticationApi.updateAuthentication(tenantId, authentication),
  onSuccess: async (authentication: Authentication) => {
    // Invalidate and refetch full saas-config
    await queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'saas-config'],
      refetchType: 'all',
      exact: true,
    });
    // Update specific sub-query cache
    queryClient.setQueryData(['tenants', tenantId, 'saas-config', 'authentication'], authentication);
    // Show success notification
    setNotification({ level: 'success', message: '...' });
  },
})
```

**Activation Flow:**

File: `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/use-activate-saas-config/useActivateSaasConfig.ts`

```typescript
useMutation({
  mutationFn: (tenantId: string) => saasConfigApi.activateSaasConfig(tenantId),
  onSuccess: async (configActivation: ConfigActivation, tenantId: string) => {
    // Invalidate and refetch config-activations
    await queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: ['tenants', tenantId, 'config-activations'],
      refetchType: 'all',
    });
  },
})
```

### 7. **Configuration Domain Models**

**Files:**
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/domain/saasConfig.ts` - Full wizard config type
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/domain/configActivation.ts` - Activation tracking
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/domain/tenantDeployment.ts` - Deployment state

**ConfigActivation Types:**
```typescript
type ConfigActivation = {
  status: ConfigActivationStatus;
  activationDate: Date;
  statusUpdatedDate?: Date;
  config: Omit<Config, 'id'>;
}

enum ConfigActivationStatus {
  Pending = 'Pending',
  Progressing = 'Progressing',
  Success = 'Success',
  Failed = 'Failed',
  RejectedInvalidConfig = 'RejectedInvalidConfig',
  RejectedUnsupportedVersion = 'RejectedUnsupportedVersion',
}

function isConfigActivationOngoing(configActivation): boolean {
  return status === Pending || status === Progressing;
}
```

**TenantDeployment Helper Functions:**
```typescript
isDeploymentAvailable(deployment) // state === RUNNING && health === HEALTHY
isDeploymentRunning(deployment)
isDeploymentHealthy(deployment)
hasLoginUrl(deployment)
hasConfig(deployment)
hasDeployment(deployment)
canLoginappBeOpened(deployment)
hasAdminappMtlsEnabled(deployment)
```

### 8. **Modal Components for Configuration**

Each modal follows the same pattern:
- Fetches current config data with `useSuspenseQuery`
- Uses React Hook Form for form state
- Updates via mutation hooks
- Closes on successful save

**Modals Located At:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/config-wizard/modals/`

Key modals:
- `ApplicationSettingsModal` - Portal or OIDC app config
- `AuthenticationSettingsModal` - Password, passkey, or OTP flows + email translations
- `UserOnboardingModal` - Manual creation or self-registration with allowed domains
- `UserExperienceSettingsModal` - Branding (colors, logo), translations, preview
- `CreateTestUserModal` - Create temporary test user in deployment

### 9. **Wizard Enable/Disable Toggle**

**Files:**
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/tenants/base/WizardDeActivationCard.tsx`
- `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/features/tenants/base/WizardDeActivationModal.tsx`

**Functionality:**
- Toggle wizard on/off via `isWizardEnabled` tenant property
- Uses `useUpdateTenant` mutation
- Card shows different label based on current state ("Unlock" vs "Revert")
- Invalidates `['isWizardEnabled', tenantId]` query cache on success

### 10. **No Configuration Comparison**

**Finding:** There is **NO existing mechanism for comparing configurations**. The wizard:
- Only shows current state preview in action items
- Does not store draft/previous versions for comparison
- Does not show "what changed" indicators
- All changes are written directly to the backend

### 11. **API Wiring**

**Main API File:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/management-ui/src/api/saas-config/saasConfig.api.ts`

```typescript
class SaasConfigApi {
  getSaasConfig(tenantId: string): Promise<SaasConfig>
  activateSaasConfig(tenantId: string): Promise<ConfigActivation>
  getConfigZip(tenantId: string): Promise<{ blob: Blob; filename: string }>
}
```

Sub-APIs for each configuration section:
- `/api/saas-config/applications/applications.api.ts`
- `/api/saas-config/authentication/authentication.api.ts`
- `/api/saas-config/ui-customization/uiCustomizationApi.ts`
- `/api/saas-config/user-onboarding/userOnboarding.api.ts`

---

## Summary

The Getting Started Wizard is implemented as a **guided configuration UI with action-based steps** rather than traditional wizard steps. It uses:

- **React Query** for polling deployment/activation status
- **React Hook Form** for modal forms with no cross-modal state
- **TanStack Query mutations** for updates with automatic cache invalidation
- **Polling hooks** to track long-running activation processes
- **Status badges and icons** to show deployment readiness
- **Direct links** to adminapp and loginapp for testing

The wizard currently **lacks configuration change tracking and comparison** - all changes are immediate backend updates.</result>

## Getting Started Wizard Backend Implementation - Complete Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the Getting Started Wizard backend implementation:

---

### 1. WIZARD CONFIGURATION DATA MODEL

The wizard configuration is based on a **SaasConfig** aggregate with modular domain model structure:

**Main Model Classes:**

- **`SaasConfig`** (`/applications/base/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/base/domain/model/saasconfig/SaasConfig.kt` - lines 13-46)
  - Properties:
    - `id: SaasConfigId` - Unique identifier
    - `tenantId: TenantId` - Associated tenant
    - `organizationId: OrganizationId` - Associated organization
    - `saasConfigData: SaasConfigData` - The actual configuration data
  - Methods for updating sub-components:
    - `updateApplication(application: Application)`
    - `updateAuthentication(authentication: Authentication)`
    - `updateUiCustomization(customization: UiCustomization)`
    - `updateUserOnboarding(userOnboarding: UserOnboarding)`

- **`SaasConfigData`** (`/applications/base/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/base/domain/model/saasconfig/SaasConfigData.kt` - lines 7-24)
  - Properties:
    - `var application: Application` - Portal or OIDC application settings
    - `var authentication: Authentication` - Password policy, email OTP, passkey settings
    - `var uiCustomization: UiCustomization` - Branding (default or custom), colors, images, language
    - `var userOnboarding: UserOnboarding` - Manual account creation or self-registration
  - Factory method: `SaasConfigData.initial()` - Creates initial configuration with defaults

**Related Domain Models:**

- **`Application`** (sealed interface in `/applications/base/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/base/domain/model/saasconfig/Application.kt`)
  - `OidcApplication` - OAuth/OIDC settings with clientId, clientSecret, redirectUri, PKCE enforcement
  - `PortalApplication` - Simple portal application (default)

- **`Authentication`** (`Authentication.kt` - lines 12-16)
  - `passwordPolicy: PasswordPolicy` - Character sets, blacklist enforcement
  - `emailOtp: EmailOtp?` - Optional email-based one-time passwords
  - `usePasskey: Boolean` - Passkey/WebAuthn support flag

- **Enumeration: `PasswordCharacterSet`** (lines 23-26)
  - `DEFAULT`, `ENHANCED`

---

### 2. TRANSFORMER MODULE (Config → ZIP Conversion)

**Entry Point:**
- **`TransformerService`** (`/applications/airlock-iam-transformer-api/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/iam/transformer/api/TransformerService.kt` - lines 18-54)
  - `fun transform(saasConfig: SaasConfigData, configSecrets: ConfigSecrets, isProductionServiceLevel: Boolean): ByteArrayOutputStream`
  - Orchestrates the complete transformation pipeline:
    1. Generates translations from config via `TranslationConfig.generate(saasConfig)`
    2. Transforms UI customizations/styles via `StylesBuildTransformer.transform()`
    3. Converts to medusa XML via `SaasConfigTransformer.transformToXml(SaasConfigArguments)`
    4. Generates SAAS YAML via `SaasConfigTransformer.transformToSaasYaml(saasConfig)`
    5. Packages all files into ZIP via `ZipCreator.zip()`

**Core Transformer:**
- **`SaasConfigTransformer`** (`/applications/airlock-iam-transformer-api/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/iam/transformer/api/SaasConfigTransformer.kt` - lines 21-54)
  - `fun transformToSaasYaml(saasConfig: SaasConfigData): String` (lines 23-26)
    - Uses `EgressTrafficPolicyFactory.create()` and `SaasYamlComponentSerializer.serialize()`
  - `fun transformToXml(saasConfigArguments: SaasConfigArguments): String` (lines 28-38)
    - Creates IAM configuration via `IamConfig.createFrom(saasConfigArguments).generateXml()`
    - Sets variable placeholders in XML properties
    - Validates XML with `IamXmlValidator().validate()`

**ZIP Creation:**
- **`ZipCreator`** (`/applications/airlock-iam-transformer/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/iam/transformer/zip/ZipCreator.kt` - lines 15-67)
  - `fun zip(medusaXml: String, translationMap: TranslationMap, saasConfig: SaasConfigData, saasYaml: String, instanceProperties: ByteArray, stylesZip: ByteArray): ByteArrayOutputStream` (lines 17-36)
  - Creates ZIP with entries:
    - `medusa-configuration.xml` - Main IAM configuration
    - `saas.yaml` - SaaS-specific configuration
    - `instance.properties` - Instance-specific properties
    - `airlock-iam-loginapp-rest-ui-customizations.zip` - UI customizations
    - `loginapp-texts/strings_{language}.properties` - Translations (per language)
    - `pwd-black-list.txt` - Optional password blacklist (if enabled)

---

### 3. PERSISTENCE LAYER (MongoDB Collections & Repositories)

**MongoDB Collections:**

- **`configService.saasConfigs`**
  - Document: `SaasConfigDocument` (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/infrastructure/repository/document/SaasConfigDocument.kt` - lines 20-31)
    - Indexed fields:
      - `@Id val id: SaasConfigId`
      - `@Indexed(unique = true) val tenantId: TenantId` - One config per tenant
      - `val organizationId: OrganizationId`
      - `var saasConfigData: SaasConfigDataDocument`
      - `val createdDate: Instant?, lastModifiedDate: Instant?, deletedDate: Instant?` - Audit trail
      - `@Version var version: Long?` - Optimistic locking

  - Sub-document: `SaasConfigDataDocument` (contains Application, Authentication, UiCustomization, UserOnboarding)
  - Compound index on `(tenantId, createdDate DESC)` for sorted queries

- **`configService.configActivations`**
  - Document: `ConfigActivationDocument` (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/infrastructure/repository/document/ConfigActivationDocument.kt` - lines 18-32)
    - `@Id val id: ConfigActivationId`
    - `val tenantId: TenantId`
    - `val organizationId: OrganizationId`
    - `val config: ConfigActivationConfigDocument` - Config metadata (id, author, name, description, uploadDate, variant)
    - `val activationDate: Instant` - When activation was requested
    - `val author: AuthorDocument` - Who triggered activation
    - `val status: ConfigActivationStatusDocument` - PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, FAILED
    - `val statusUpdatedDate: Instant?` - When status last changed
    - `val createdDate, lastModifiedDate, deletedDate: Instant?` - Audit trail
    - `@Version var version: Long?` - Optimistic locking

**Repository Interfaces:**

- **`SaasConfigRepository`** (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/domain/repository/SaasConfigRepository.kt` - lines 11-16)
  - `fun save(config: SaasConfig): SaasConfig`
  - `fun getByTenantId(tenantId: TenantId): SaasConfig`
  - `fun deleteByTenantId(tenantId: TenantId)`
  - `fun verifySecretNotInUse(tenantId: TenantId, secretId: SecretId)`

- **`ConfigActivationRepository`** (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/domain/repository/ConfigActivationRepository.kt`)
  - `fun save(configActivation: ConfigActivation): ConfigActivation`
  - `fun getById(configActivationId: ConfigActivationId): ConfigActivation`
  - `fun findById(configActivationId: ConfigActivationId): ConfigActivation?`
  - `fun findByTenantId(tenantId: TenantId): List<ConfigActivation>`
  - `fun findByStatus(status: ConfigActivationStatus): List<ConfigActivation>`
  - `fun findByTenantIdAndStatus(tenantId: TenantId, status: ConfigActivationStatus): ConfigActivation?`

**Repository Implementations:**

- **`SaasConfigRepositoryImpl`** (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/infrastructure/repository/SaasConfigRepositoryImpl.kt`)
  - Uses `SecurityAwareMongoTemplate` for secured queries
  - Save logic (lines 38-50): Creates or merges using `SaasConfigDocumentMapper`
  - Includes nested criteria checking for secret usage verification

- **`ConfigActivationRepositoryImpl`** (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/infrastructure/repository/ConfigActivationRepositoryImpl.kt` - lines 22-108)
  - Uses `SecurityAwareMongoTemplate`
  - Filters deleted records: `Criteria.where(ConfigActivationDocument::deletedDate.name).`is`(null)`
  - Sorts by creation date descending for history queries
  - Supports status-based queries for deployment pipeline tracking

---

### 4. ACTIVATION/DEPLOYMENT PIPELINE

**Flow Diagram:**
```
User (Frontend) 
  → GET /saas-config (read wizard config)
  → POST /saas-config/zip (download generated config)
  → POST /saas-config/activation (initiate deployment)
    → ConfigActivationService.activateSaasConfig()
      → Creates Config aggregate from transformed ZIP
      → ConfigActivationService.activateConfig()
        → Creates ConfigActivation (status=PENDING)
        → Publishes ConfigActivatedEvent
          → ConfigActivationEventListener (async)
            → DeploymentConfigService.createDeploymentConfig()
              → Creates deployment configuration
              → Updates status to DEPLOYED/COMPLETED
```

**Key Service Classes:**

- **`SaasConfigService`** (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/application/service/SaasConfigService.kt` - lines 42-164)
  
  - `fun getSaasConfig(tenantId: TenantId): SaasConfig` (lines 57, @HasConfigActivatePermissions)
  
  - `fun transformSaasConfigToZip(tenantId: TenantId): ConfigZipFile` (lines 62-71)
    - Retrieves tenant config and transforms to ZIP bytes
    - Returns `ConfigZipFile` with byte content
  
  - `fun transformSaasConfig(tenantId: TenantId): ByteArray` (lines 76-96)
    - Gets SaasConfig from repository
    - Retrieves required secrets: ENCRYPTION_KEY, HMAC_KEY, LOGIN_FROM_NEW_DEVICE_KEY
    - Calls `TransformerService.transform()` with:
      - `saasConfig.saasConfigData`
      - `ConfigSecrets` (file references to secret storage)
      - `tenant.isProductionServiceLevel` (determines deployment config)
  
  - `fun addInitialSaasConfig(tenantId: TenantId): SaasConfig` (lines 101-116, @HasTenantCreatePermissions)
    - Creates initial config with defaults via `SaasConfigData.initial()`
    - Stores in MongoDB
  
  - `fun activateSaasConfig(tenantId: TenantId): ConfigActivation` (lines 121-145, @HasConfigActivatePermissions)
    - Creates a `Config` aggregate with type STANDARD (not TEST/SAMPLE)
    - Name: "Getting started Config"
    - Description: "This config was created in Getting started"
    - Calls `ConfigActivationService.activateConfig()` to trigger deployment

- **`ConfigActivationService`** (`/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/application/service/ConfigActivationService.kt` - lines 42-164)
  
  - `fun activateConfig(tenantId: TenantId, configId: ConfigId): ConfigActivation` (lines 58-119)
    - Creates `ConfigActivation` aggregate with status PENDING
    - Retrieves associated config and secrets/key pairs
    - Saves to `ConfigActivationRepository`
    - **Publishes `ConfigActivatedEvent`** with:
      - Config ZIP content
      - All tenant secrets and key pairs
      - Egress traffic policy
    - Event is asynchronously consumed by `ConfigActivationEventListener`
  
  - `fun updateConfigActivationStatus(configActivationId: ConfigActivationId, status: ConfigActivationStatus)` (lines 124-147)
    - Called by deployment service to update activation status
    - Records status change timestamp
  
  - `fun getConfigActivationHistory(tenantId: TenantId): List<ConfigActivation>` (lines 152-155, read-only)
    - Returns all config activations for a tenant, sorted by creation date DESC

**API Controller:**

- **`ConfigController`** (`/applications/api-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/api/service/infrastructure/controller/ConfigController.kt` - lines 30-114)
  
  - `override fun getConfigZip(tenantId: String): ResponseEntity<Resource>` (lines 95-103)
    - Calls `SaasConfigService.transformSaasConfigToZip()`
    - Returns ByteArrayResource with ZIP content
    - Headers: `Content-Type: application/zip`, `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="config-{tenantId}-{timestamp}.zip"`
  
  - `override fun activateSaasConfig(tenantId: String): ResponseEntity<ConfigActivationResponseDto>` (lines 88-93)
    - Calls `SaasConfigService.activateSaasConfig()`
    - Returns HTTP 202 ACCEPTED with ConfigActivation DTO

**Event-Driven Deployment:**

- **`ConfigActivatedEvent`** (`/applications/config-service-api/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/domain/event/ConfigActivatedEvent.kt` - lines 17-63)
  - Properties:
    - `configActivationId: ConfigActivationId`
    - `tenantId: TenantId`
    - `configId: ConfigId`
    - `content: ByteArray` - The generated ZIP content
    - `createdDate: Instant`
    - `secrets: Set<EventSecret>` - Name + SecretRef
    - `keyPairs: Set<EventKeyPair>` - Public key, storage location, alias, certificate

- **`ConfigActivationEventListener`** (`/applications/deployment-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/deployment/service/infrastructure/event/ConfigActivationEventListener.kt` - lines 17-39)
  - `@EventListener fun onConfigActivated(event: ConfigActivatedEvent)` (lines 20-38)
  - Asynchronously creates deployment configuration
  - Calls `DeploymentConfigService.createDeploymentConfig()` with secrets and key pairs

---

### 5. WIZARD PERSISTENCE FLOW

**Creating Initial Wizard Config:**
1. During tenant creation: `SaasConfigService.addInitialSaasConfig(tenantId)`
2. Creates `SaasConfig` aggregate with initial `SaasConfigData`
3. Repository persists to `configService.saasConfigs` collection

**Updating Wizard Config (via Frontend Modals):**
1. User updates field in UI (Application, Authentication, UICustomization, UserOnboarding)
2. Frontend calls API endpoint to update specific field
3. `SaasConfigService` retrieves current config from repository
4. Calls update method: `config.updateApplication()`, `config.updateAuthentication()`, etc.
5. Repository saves updated config:
   - If new: Inserts new document
   - If existing: Merges into existing using mapper (preserves version for optimistic locking)

**Downloading Config ZIP:**
1. Frontend calls `GET /saas-config/zip`
2. `ConfigController.getConfigZip(tenantId)` handler
3. `SaasConfigService.transformSaasConfigToZip()`:
   - Retrieves SaasConfig from `configService.saasConfigs`
   - Loads required secrets from vault
   - Calls `TransformerService.transform()`
   - Returns ByteArray wrapped in ConfigZipFile
4. Controller streams ZIP as downloadable file

**Activating Config for Deployment:**
1. Frontend calls `POST /saas-config/activation`
2. `ConfigController.activateSaasConfig(tenantId)` handler
3. `SaasConfigService.activateSaasConfig()`:
   - Transforms current SaasConfig to ZIP bytes

### 6. CHANGE DETECTION & DIFF MECHANISMS

**Current State: Limited/Implicit Diff Support**

The codebase does NOT have explicit diff or change-detection mechanisms. However, the design supports implicit comparisons:

  - `@LastModifiedDate val lastModifiedDate: Instant?` - When last updated
  - `@Version var version: Long?` - Optimistic locking (incremented on each save)
  - `val deletedDate: Instant?` - Soft delete marker

- `useActivateSaasConfig()` invalidates `['tenants', tenantId, 'config-activations']` query

     - saas.yaml (SaaS-specific config)
     - translation maps (server & UI)
     - instance.properties
     - styles ZIP (UI customizations)

  ```kotlin
  data class ConfigActivatedEvent(
      val configActivationId: ConfigActivationId,
      val tenantId: TenantId,
      val configId: ConfigId,
      val content: ByteArray,                    // Zipped config
      val createdDate: Instant,
      val secrets: Set<EventSecret>,            // Referenced secrets
      val keyPairs: Set<EventKeyPair>,          // Referenced key pairs
      val egressTrafficPolicy: EgressTrafficPolicy  // Networking config
  )
  ```

**ConfigActivationEventListener**
- **File Path:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/deployment-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/deployment/service/infrastructure/event/ConfigActivationEventListener.kt`
- **Behavior:**
  ```kotlin
  @EventListener
  fun onConfigActivated(event: ConfigActivatedEvent) {
      deploymentConfigService.createDeploymentConfig(
          DeploymentConfigId(event.configActivationId.value),
          DeploymentId(event.tenantId.value),
          event.content,
          event.secrets.map { DeploymentSecret(...) },
          event.keyPairs.map { DeploymentKeyPair(...) },
          event.egressTrafficPolicy
      )
  }
  ```

**Activation Sequence:**
1. ConfigActivationService.activateConfig() → Creates ConfigActivation (PENDING), publishes ConfigActivatedEvent
2. ConfigActivationEventListener receives event → Creates DeploymentConfig
3. DeploymentConfigService publishes DeploymentConfigUpdatedEvent
4. Cloud Operator (external) processes DeploymentConfig and updates status
5. DeploymentService.updateDeploymentStatus() → Updates Deployment, publishes DeploymentUpdatedEvent

Key audit actions in wizard flow:
- `CONFIG_UPLOAD` - When config is uploaded
- `CONFIG_ACTIVATION_CREATE` - When config activation is created
- `CONFIG_ACTIVATION_UPDATE` - When activation status is updated
- `SAAS_CONFIG_CREATE` - Initial SaaS config creation
- `SAAS_CONFIG_ACTIVATE` - When SaaS config is activated via wizard
- `SAAS_CONFIG_UPDATE` - When application/authentication/onboarding settings updated in wizard
- `DEPLOYMENT_CREATE` - When deployment is created
- `DEPLOYMENT_CONFIG_CREATE` - When deployment config is created

### **SUMMARY: GETTING STARTED WIZARD FLOW**

1. **Initialization:** `SaasConfigService.addInitialSaasConfig()` creates initial config
2. **Wizard Updates:** 
   - User updates Application → `ApplicationService.updateApplication()`
   - User updates Authentication → `AuthenticationService.updateAuthentication()`
   - User updates UserOnboarding → `UserOnboardingService.updateUserOnboarding()`
3. **Config Download:** `ConfigController.getConfigZip()` → `SaasConfigService.transformSaasConfigToZip()`
4. **Activation:** 
   - `ConfigController.activateSaasConfig()` → 
   - `SaasConfigService.activateSaasConfig()` → 
   - Creates Config via `ConfigService.addConfigToTenant()` →
   - Activates via `ConfigActivationService.activateConfig()` →
   - Publishes `ConfigActivatedEvent`
5. **Deployment:**
   - `ConfigActivationEventListener.onConfigActivated()` →
   - `DeploymentConfigService.createDeploymentConfig()` →
   - Cloud Operator processes and updates status

All of these components work together to provide a complete getting started wizard experience with configuration transformation and deployment orchestration.</result>

  3. **SaasConfigTransformer.transformToXml()** - Converts SaaS config to IAM medusa.xml
  4. **SaasConfigTransformer.transformToSaasYaml()** - Generates saas.yaml with egress traffic policy
  5. **ZipCreator.zip()** - Creates final deployment ZIP containing:
     - medusa.xml (IAM configuration)
     - saas.yaml (SaaS-specific config)
     - translation maps (server & UI)
     - instance.properties
     - styles ZIP (UI customizations)

**SaasConfigTransformer**
- **File Path:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/airlock-iam-transformer-api/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/iam/transformer/api/SaasConfigTransformer.kt`

  ```kotlin
  fun transformToSaasYaml(saasConfig: SaasConfigData): String
  ```
- **Process:**
  - Creates IamConfig from SaasConfigArguments
  - Validates XML with IamXmlValidator
  - Replaces variable placeholders in properties

### **4. EVENT-DRIVEN ACTIVATION PIPELINE**

**ConfigActivatedEvent**
- **File Path:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/config-service-api/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/domain/event/ConfigActivatedEvent.kt`
- **Structure:**
  ```kotlin
  data class ConfigActivatedEvent(
      val configActivationId: ConfigActivationId,
      val tenantId: TenantId,
      val configId: ConfigId,
      val content: ByteArray,                    // Zipped config
      val createdDate: Instant,
      val secrets: Set<EventSecret>,            // Referenced secrets
      val keyPairs: Set<EventKeyPair>,          // Referenced key pairs
      val egressTrafficPolicy: EgressTrafficPolicy  // Networking config
  )
  ```

**ConfigActivationEventListener**
- **File Path:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/deployment-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/deployment/service/infrastructure/event/ConfigActivationEventListener.kt`
- **Behavior:**
  ```kotlin
  @EventListener
  fun onConfigActivated(event: ConfigActivatedEvent) {
      deploymentConfigService.createDeploymentConfig(
          DeploymentConfigId(event.configActivationId.value),
          DeploymentId(event.tenantId.value),
          event.content,
          event.secrets.map { DeploymentSecret(...) },
          event.keyPairs.map { DeploymentKeyPair(...) },
          event.egressTrafficPolicy
      )
  }
  ```

**Activation Sequence:**
1. ConfigActivationService.activateConfig() → Creates ConfigActivation (PENDING), publishes ConfigActivatedEvent
2. ConfigActivationEventListener receives event → Creates DeploymentConfig
3. DeploymentConfigService publishes DeploymentConfigUpdatedEvent
4. Cloud Operator (external) processes DeploymentConfig and updates status
5. DeploymentService.updateDeploymentStatus() → Updates Deployment, publishes DeploymentUpdatedEvent

---

### **5. DTOs & REQUEST-RESPONSE OBJECTS**

**Configuration-Related DTOs:**
- `ConfigResponseDto` - Single config response
- `ConfigsResponseDto` - List of configs
- `ConfigActivationResponseDto` - Activation status response
- `ConfigActivationsResponseDto` - List of activations
- `ConfigActivationCreateRequestDto` - Request to activate config
- `ConfigAttributesDto` - Config metadata
- `ConfigActivationStatusDto` - Activation status (PENDING, PROGRESSING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, etc.)

**SaaS Config DTOs:**
- `SaasConfigResponseDto` - Current SaaS configuration
- `SaasConfigAttributesDto` - Configuration details
- `UserOnboardingResponseDto` / `UserOnboardingRequestDto` - Onboarding settings
- `ApplicationResponseDto` / `ApplicationRequestDto` - Application config (OIDC or Portal)
- `UiCustomizationDto` - UI customization settings
- `OidcAuthorityDto` - OIDC authority information

**Mapper Classes:**
- `ConfigActivationDtoMapper` - Maps between ConfigActivation domain and DTO
- `ConfigDtoMapper` - Maps Config domain to DTO
- `SaasConfigDtoMapper` - Maps SaasConfig to DTO
- `UserOnboardingDtoMapper` - Maps UserOnboarding to DTO
- `ApplicationDtoMapper` - Maps Application to DTO

---

### **6. DOMAIN MODELS**

**ConfigActivation**
- **File Path:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/config-service/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/config/service/domain/model/ConfigActivation.kt`
- **Fields:**
  ```kotlin
  val id: ConfigActivationId
  val tenantId: TenantId
  val organizationId: OrganizationId
  val config: ConfigActivationConfig
  val activationDate: Instant
  val author: Author
  var status: ConfigActivationStatus  // PENDING, PROGRESSING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, etc.
  var statusUpdatedDate: Instant?
  ```
- **Status Enum:** PENDING → PROGRESSING → SUCCEEDED (or FAILED, CANCELLED, SKIPPED, etc.)

**SaasConfig**
- **File Path:** `/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2/applications/base/src/main/kotlin/com/airlock/saas/base/domain/model/saasconfig/SaasConfig.kt`
- **Contains:** SaasConfigData with Application, Authentication, UiCustomization, UserOnboarding

**DeploymentConfig**
- Represents the config prepared for deployment
- Contains secrets, key pairs, egress traffic policy
- Created when ConfigActivatedEvent is published

---

### **7. SECURITY & PERMISSIONS**

**Authorization Annotations:**
- `@IsAdmin` - Admin access required (ConfigController, ConfigActivationController, ApplicationController)
- `@IsCloudOperator` - Cloud operator access (DeploymentController, DeploymentConfigController)
- `@HasConfigActivatePermissions` - Permission to activate configs (service methods)
- `@HasConfigUploadPermissions` - Permission to upload configs
- `@HasDeploymentsManagePermissions` - Permission to manage deployments

---

### **8. AUDIT LOGGING**

Key audit actions in wizard flow:
- `CONFIG_UPLOAD` - When config is uploaded
- `CONFIG_ACTIVATION_CREATE` - When config activation is created
- `CONFIG_ACTIVATION_UPDATE` - When activation status is updated
- `SAAS_CONFIG_CREATE` - Initial SaaS config creation
- `SAAS_CONFIG_ACTIVATE` - When SaaS config is activated via wizard
- `SAAS_CONFIG_UPDATE` - When application/authentication/onboarding settings updated in wizard
- `DEPLOYMENT_CREATE` - When deployment is created
- `DEPLOYMENT_CONFIG_CREATE` - When deployment config is created

---

### **SUMMARY: GETTING STARTED WIZARD FLOW**

1. **Initialization:** `SaasConfigService.addInitialSaasConfig()` creates initial config
2. **Wizard Updates:** 
   - User updates Application → `ApplicationService.updateApplication()`
   - User updates Authentication → `AuthenticationService.updateAuthentication()`
   - User updates UserOnboarding → `UserOnboardingService.updateUserOnboarding()`
3. **Config Download:** `ConfigController.getConfigZip()` → `SaasConfigService.transformSaasConfigToZip()`
4. **Activation:** 
   - `ConfigController.activateSaasConfig()` → 
   - `SaasConfigService.activateSaasConfig()` → 
   - Creates Config via `ConfigService.addConfigToTenant()` →
   - Activates via `ConfigActivationService.activateConfig()` →
   - Publishes `ConfigActivatedEvent`
5. **Deployment:**
   - `ConfigActivationEventListener.onConfigActivated()` →
   - `DeploymentConfigService.createDeploymentConfig()` →
   - Cloud Operator processes and updates status

All of these components work together to provide a complete getting started wizard experience with configuration transformation and deployment orchestration.</result>

{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2","sessionId":"54ae9f77-7d07-4689-a92e-9df615f90ef0","version":"2.1.49","gitBranch":"MINI-configure-claude-code","agentId":"acfe7b1","slug":"steady-zooming-hartmanis","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Explore the codebase to understand the configuration and deployment data model in depth. Focus on:

1. How tenant configurations (ZIP files) are stored - MongoDB documents, blob storage, etc.
2. The "Config" entity/document - what fields it has, how it tracks versions
3. The "Deployment" entity/document - how it references configurations
4. The activation pipeline - from config upload/creation to deployment
5. How the system tracks which configuration is "active" or "running" on a tenant
6. Any versioning or history mechanism for configurations
7. The relationship between wizard config, ZIP config, and deployed config

Search in `applications/` for:
- Config-related entities, repositories, services
- Deployment-related entities, repositories, services  
- Tenant management code
- Any audit/history mechanisms

    16→
    17→@Aggregate
    18→class DeploymentConfig(
    19→	val id: DeploymentConfigId,
    20→	val deploymentId: DeploymentId,
    21→
    22→	val secrets: List<DeploymentSecret>,
    23→	val keyPairs: List<DeploymentKeyPair>,
    24→
    25→	adminapp: AdminappModule = AdminappModule(),
    26→	loginapp: LoginappModule = LoginappModule(),
    27→	serviceContainer: ServiceContainerModule = ServiceContainerModule(),
    28→
    29→	// TODO ALSAAS-4426: Consider including zipFileContent directly
    30→
    31→	status: DeploymentConfigStatus = PENDING,
    32→	createdDate: Instant = Instant.now(),
    33→	val deletedDate: Instant? = null,
    34→) {
    35→
    36→	var adminapp: AdminappModule = adminapp
    37→		private set
    38→
    39→	var loginapp: LoginappModule = loginapp
    40→		private set
    41→
    42→	var serviceContainer: ServiceContainerModule = serviceContainer
    43→		private set
    44→
    45→	val createdDate: Instant = createdDate.truncatedTo(ChronoUnit.MILLIS)
    46→
    47→	var status: DeploymentConfigStatus = status
    48→		private set
    49→
    50→	fun updateStatus(newStatus: DeploymentConfigStatus): DeploymentConfig =
    51→		apply {
    52→			when {
    53→				status.isOngoing -> status = newStatus
    54→				status == newStatus -> {
    55→					// we're already in the desired status
    56→				}
    57→
    58→				else -> {
    59→					logger
    60→						.atWarn()
    61→						.setMessage("Attempted to change from terminal state $status to another terminal state: $newStatus")
    62→						.addKeyValue(DEPLOYMENT_CONFIG_ID, id.value)
    63→					throw illegalModification()
    64→				}
    65→			}
    66→		}
    67→
    68→	fun markSkipped(): DeploymentConfig =
    69→		apply {
    70→			if (status.isOngoing) {
    71→				status = DeploymentConfigStatus.SKIPPED
    72→			}
    73→		}
    74→
    75→	override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    76→		if (this === other) return true
    77→		if (other !is DeploymentConfig) return false
    78→
    79→		return id == other.id
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	override fun hashCode(): Int = id.hashCode()
    83→}
    84→
    85→private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DeploymentConfig::class.java)
    86→"}]},"uuid":"067dc011-31f8-4c17-a69a-6dd7f8c636a0","timestamp":"2026-02-20T15:01:33.810Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"eb957d3d-2759-46e7-afb0-4385d427a3f2"}

     5→package com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.model
     6→
     7→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.Author
     8→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.ValidationWithException.notBlankOrLongerThan
     9→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.ValidationWithException.required
    10→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.exceptions.DomainValidationException.Companion.invalidValue
    11→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.stereotypes.Aggregate
    12→import com.airlock.saas.ids.ConfigId
    13→import com.airlock.saas.ids.ConfigZipFileId
    14→import com.airlock.saas.ids.OrganizationId
    15→import com.airlock.saas.ids.TenantId
    16→import java.time.Instant
    17→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
    18→
    19→@Aggregate
    20→class Config(
    21→	val id: ConfigId = ConfigId.random(),
    22→	val tenantId: TenantId,
    23→	val organizationId: OrganizationId,
    24→	val author: Author,
    25→	val name: String?,
    26→	val description: String?,
    27→	uploadDate: Instant,
    28→	val variant: ConfigVariant = ConfigVariant.STANDARD,
    29→	val configZipFileId: ConfigZipFileId,
    30→) {
    31→
    32→	val uploadDate: Instant = uploadDate.truncatedTo(ChronoUnit.MILLIS)
    33→
    34→	init {
    35→		// Name may be `null` if it is one of the system configs
    36→		if (variant.isSystemConfig()) {
    37→			if (name != null) {
    38→				throw invalidValue(::name.name)
    39→			}
    40→		} else {
    41→			required(::name)
    42→		}
    43→
    44→		notBlankOrLongerThan(::name, MAX_NAME_LENGTH)
    45→		notBlankOrLongerThan(::description, MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH)
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    49→		if (this === other) return true
    50→		if (other !is Config) return false
    51→
    52→		return id == other.id
    53→	}
    54→
    55→	override fun hashCode(): Int = id.hashCode()
    56→}
    57→
    58→enum class ConfigVariant {
    59→	STANDARD,
    60→	TEST,
    61→	SAMPLE,
    62→	;
    63→
    64→	fun isSystemConfig(): Boolean = this == SAMPLE || this == TEST
    65→}
    66→
    67→private const val MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 255
    68→private const val MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH = 500
    69→"}]},"uuid":"ecc4fd46-d173-4a0a-a31d-0e606470ac4e","timestamp":"2026-02-20T15:01:38.408Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e3336cf2-4ecf-4f59-80d8-6748ffc80746"}

     5→package com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.model
     6→
     7→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.ValidationWithException.notLargerThan
     8→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.stereotypes.ValueObject
     9→import com.airlock.saas.ids.ConfigZipFileId
    10→import com.airlock.saas.ids.OrganizationId
    11→import com.airlock.saas.ids.TenantId
    12→
    13→@ValueObject
    14→data class ConfigZipFile(
    15→	val id: ConfigZipFileId = ConfigZipFileId.random(),
    16→	val tenantId: TenantId,
    17→	val organizationId: OrganizationId,
    18→	val content: ByteArray,
    19→) {
    20→
    21→	init {
    22→		notLargerThan(::content, MAX_BYTES)
    23→	}
    24→
    25→	// We need to override equals and hashcode because as value object the values of the fields should be checked for
    26→	// equality, never the reference (as it would be the case for ByteArray)
    27→	override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    28→		if (this === other) return true
    29→		if (javaClass != other?.javaClass) return false
    30→
    31→		other as ConfigZipFile
    32→
    33→		if (id != other.id) return false
    34→		if (tenantId != other.tenantId) return false
    35→		if (organizationId != other.organizationId) return false
    36→		return content.contentEquals(other.content)
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	override fun hashCode(): Int {
    40→		var result = id.hashCode()
    41→		result = 31 * result + tenantId.hashCode()
    42→		result = 31 * result + organizationId.hashCode()
    43→		result = 31 * result + content.contentHashCode()
    44→		return result
    45→	}
    46→}

{"parentUuid":"398b4971-941c-42ef-98b6-a0ffeed11e47","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/mkramer/Documents/Projects/airlock-saas-2","sessionId":"54ae9f77-7d07-4689-a92e-9df615f90ef0","version":"2.1.49","gitBranch":"MINI-configure-claude-code","agentId":"acfe7b1","slug":"steady-zooming-hartmanis","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019xoBCmhrJmX4ThZK4izguW","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/*

     4→
     5→package com.airlock.saas.config.service.application.service
     6→
     7→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.audit.AuditAction
     8→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.audit.AuditParam
     9→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.audit.NoAuditLogRequired
    10→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.audit.WithAuditLog
    11→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.logging.CONFIG_ACTIVATION_ID
    12→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.logging.CONFIG_ACTIVATION_STATUS
    13→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.logging.CONFIG_ID
    14→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.logging.CONFIG_TYPE
    15→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.logging.TENANT_ID
    16→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.security.HasDeploymentsManagePermissions
    17→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.service.TransactionalService
    18→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.security.ActingUserProvider
    19→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.model.toAuthor
    20→import com.airlock.saas.base.domain.security.HasConfigActivatePermissions
    21→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.event.ConfigActivatedEvent
    22→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.event.EventKeyPair
    23→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.event.EventSecret
    24→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.model.ConfigActivation
    25→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.model.ConfigActivationConfig
    26→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.model.ConfigActivationStatus
    27→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.repository.ConfigActivationRepository
    28→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.repository.ConfigRepository
    29→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.repository.ConfigZipFileRepository
    30→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.repository.KeyPairRepository
    31→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.repository.SecretRepository
    32→import com.airlock.saas.config.service.domain.repository.TenantRepository
    33→import com.airlock.saas.ids.ConfigActivationId
    34→import com.airlock.saas.ids.ConfigId
    35→import com.airlock.saas.ids.TenantId
    36→import io.opentelemetry.instrumentation.annotations.WithSpan
    37→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    38→import org.springframework.context.ApplicationEventPublisher
    39→import java.time.Clock
    40→
    41→@TransactionalService
    42→class ConfigActivationService(
    43→	private val publisher: ApplicationEventPublisher,
    44→	private val clock: Clock,
    45→	private val configActivationRepository: ConfigActivationRepository,
    46→	private val configRepository: ConfigRepository,
    47→	private val configZipFileRepository: ConfigZipFileRepository,
    48→	private val actingUserProvider: ActingUserProvider,
    49→	private val secretRepository: SecretRepository,
    50→	private val keyPairRepository: KeyPairRepository,
    51→	private val tenantRepository: TenantRepository,
    52→) {
    53→
    54→	@WithAuditLog(AuditAction.CONFIG_ACTIVATION_CREATE, "Created configuration activation")
    55→	@HasConfigActivatePermissions
    56→	@Suppress("LongMethod")
    57→	@WithSpan
    58→	fun activateConfig(@AuditParam tenantId: TenantId, @AuditParam configId: ConfigId): ConfigActivation {
    59→		if (actingUserProvider.actingAdminOrClient.isActingOauthClient()) {
    60→			tenantRepository.verifyIsNotProductionServiceLevel(tenantId)
    61→		}
    62→
    63→		val config = configRepository.getById(configId)
    64→		val configZipFile = configZipFileRepository.getById(config.configZipFileId)
    65→
    66→		val actingAdminOrClient = actingUserProvider.actingAdminOrClient
    67→		val configActivation = ConfigActivation.create(
    68→			tenantId,
    69→			actingAdminOrClient.organizationId,
    70→			ConfigActivationConfig(
    71→				id = config.id,
    72→				author = config.author,
    73→				name = config.name,
    74→				description = config.description,
    75→				uploadDate = config.uploadDate,
    76→				variant = config.variant,
    77→			),
    78→			clock.instant(),
    79→			actingAdminOrClient.toAuthor(),
    80→		)
    81→
    82→		val secrets = secretRepository.findByTenantId(tenantId)
    83→			.mapTo(mutableSetOf()) { EventSecret(it.name, it.secretRef) }
    84→
    85→		val keyPairs = keyPairRepository.findByTenantId(tenantId)
    86→			.mapTo(mutableSetOf()) {
    87→				EventKeyPair(
    88→					publicKey = it.publicKey,
    89→					keyPairRef = it.keyPairRef,
    90→					keyStorePath = it.keyStorePath,
    91→					privateKeyAlias = it.privateKeyAlias,
    92→					certificate = it.certificate,
    93→				)
    94→			}
    95→
    96→		val egressTrafficPolicy = tenantRepository.getById(tenantId).egressTrafficPolicy()
    97→
    98→		configActivationRepository.save(configActivation)
    99→		publisher.publishEvent(
   100→			ConfigActivatedEvent(
   101→				configActivationId = configActivation.id,
   102→				tenantId = tenantId,
   103→				configId = configId,
   104→				content = configZipFile.content,
   105→				createdDate = clock.instant(),
   106→				secrets = secrets,
   107→				keyPairs = keyPairs,
   108→				egressTrafficPolicy = egressTrafficPolicy,
   109→			),
   110→		)
   111→
   112→		logger.atInfo().setMessage("Config activated")
   113→			.addKeyValue(TENANT_ID, tenantId.value)
   114→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ACTIVATION_ID, configActivation.id)
   115→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ID, config.id.value)
   116→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_TYPE, config::class.simpleName)
   117→
   118→		return configActivation
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	@WithAuditLog(AuditAction.CONFIG_ACTIVATION_UPDATE, "Updated configuration activation status")
   122→	@HasDeploymentsManagePermissions
   123→	@WithSpan
   124→	fun updateConfigActivationStatus(
   125→		@AuditParam configActivationId: ConfigActivationId,
   126→		@AuditParam status: ConfigActivationStatus,
   127→	) {
   128→		val configActivation = configActivationRepository.findById(configActivationId)
   129→
   130→		if (configActivation == null) {
   131→			logger.atInfo()
   132→				.setMessage("Config activation not found or marked for deletion and thus not updated")
   133→				.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ACTIVATION_ID, configActivationId.value)
   134→				.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ACTIVATION_STATUS, status.name).log()
   135→
   136→			return
   137→		}
   138→
   139→		configActivation.status = status
   140→		configActivation.statusUpdatedDate = clock.instant()
   141→		configActivationRepository.save(configActivation)
   142→
   143→		logger.atInfo()
   144→			.setMessage("Config activation status updated")
   145→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ACTIVATION_ID, configActivationId.value)
   146→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ACTIVATION_STATUS, status.name).log()
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	@NoAuditLogRequired(reason = "Read-only operation")
   150→	@HasConfigActivatePermissions
   151→	@WithSpan
   152→	fun getConfigActivationHistory(tenantId: TenantId): List<ConfigActivation> {
   153→		tenantRepository.verifyTenantsExist(listOf(tenantId))
   154→		return configActivationRepository.findByTenantId(tenantId)
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	@NoAuditLogRequired(reason = "Read-only operation")
   158→	@HasConfigActivatePermissions
   159→	@WithSpan
   160→	fun getById(configActivationId: ConfigActivationId): ConfigActivation =
   161→		configActivationRepository.getById(configActivationId)
   162→}
   163→
   164→private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ConfigActivationService::class.java)
   165→"}]},"uuid":"a51cc6b8-40f3-4ea3-9844-38f104525968","timestamp":"2026-02-20T15:01:55.784Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"398b4971-941c-42ef-98b6-a0ffeed11e47"}

     7→import com.airlock.saas.base.application.audit.AuditAction

    35→@TransactionalService
    36→class ConfigService(
    37→	private val clock: Clock,
    38→	private val configRepository: ConfigRepository,
    39→	private val configZipFileRepository: ConfigZipFileRepository,
    40→	private val tenantRepository: TenantRepository,
    41→	private val actingUserProvider: ActingUserProvider,
    42→) {
    43→
    44→	@WithAuditLog(AuditAction.CONFIG_UPLOAD, "Uploaded configuration")
    45→	@HasConfigUploadPermissions
    46→	@WithSpan
    47→	fun createConfig(
    48→		@AuditParam tenantId: TenantId,
    49→		@AuditParam name: String,
    50→		@AuditParam description: String?,
    51→		configContent: ByteArray,
    52→	): Config =
    53→		addConfigToTenant(
    54→			tenantId = tenantId,
    55→			configContentProvider = { configContent },
    56→			configProvider = { tenant, configZipFileId ->
    57→				Config(
    58→					tenantId = tenantId,
    59→					organizationId = tenant.organizationId,
    60→					configZipFileId = configZipFileId,
    61→					author = actingUserProvider.actingAdminOrClient.toAuthor(),
    62→					name = name,
    63→					description = description,
    64→					uploadDate = clock.instant(),
    65→				)
    66→			},
    67→		)
    68→
    69→	@HasTenantCreatePermissions
    70→	@NoAuditLogRequired(reason = "Non auditable action")
    71→	@WithSpan
    72→	fun addTestConfig(tenantId: TenantId): Config =
    73→		addConfigToTenant(
    74→			tenantId = tenantId,
    75→			configContentProvider = {
    76→				requireNotNull(javaClass.getResourceAsStream("/tenant-test-config.zip")).readAllBytes()
    77→			},
    78→			configProvider = { tenant, configZipFileId ->
    79→				Config(
    80→					tenantId = tenantId,
    81→					organizationId = tenant.organizationId,
    82→					configZipFileId = configZipFileId,
    83→					author = SystemUser.toAuthor(),
    84→					name = null,
    85→					description = null,
    86→					uploadDate = clock.instant(),
    87→					variant = ConfigVariant.TEST,
    88→				)
    89→			},
    90→		)
    91→
    92→	@HasTenantCreatePermissions
    93→	@NoAuditLogRequired(reason = "Non auditable action")
    94→	@WithSpan
    95→	fun addSampleConfig(tenantId: TenantId): Config =
    96→		addConfigToTenant(
    97→			tenantId = tenantId,
    98→			configContentProvider = {
    99→				requireNotNull(javaClass.getResourceAsStream("/tenant-sample-config.zip")).readAllBytes()
   100→			},
   101→			configProvider = { tenant, configZipFileId ->
   102→				Config(

   105→					configZipFileId = configZipFileId,

   115→	// TODO ALSAAS-4138: We opened this function so that partners may activate configurations in the
   116→	// TODO ALSAAS-4138: Getting Started Wizard. This function should not be used anywhere other than
   117→	// TODO ALSAAS-4138: this class and `activateSaasConfig` in `SaasConfigService` .
   118→	// TODO ALSAAS-4138: Make this `private` again
   119→	@NoAuditLogRequired(reason = "Callers are annotated with `@WithAuditLog`")
   120→	internal fun addConfigToTenant(
   121→		tenantId: TenantId,
   122→		configContentProvider: () -> ByteArray,
   123→		configProvider: (Tenant, ConfigZipFileId) -> Config,
   124→	): Config {
   125→		val tenant = tenantRepository.getById(tenantId)
   126→		val configZipFile = ConfigZipFile(
   127→			tenantId = tenantId,
   128→			organizationId = tenant.organizationId,
   129→			content = configContentProvider(),
   130→		)
   131→
   132→		val config = configProvider(tenant, configZipFile.id)
   133→
   134→		logger.atInfo()
   135→			.setMessage("Adding config to tenant")
   136→			.addKeyValue(TENANT_ID, tenant.id.value)
   137→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ID, config.id.value)
   138→			.addKeyValue(CONFIG_ZIP_FILE_ID, configZipFile.id.value)
   139→			.log()
   140→
   141→		configZipFileRepository.save(configZipFile)
   142→		return configRepository.save(config)
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	@NoAuditLogRequired(reason = "Read-only operation")
   146→	@HasConfigViewPermissions
   147→	@WithSpan
   148→	fun getConfigs(tenantId: TenantId): List<Config> {
   149→		val tenant = tenantRepository.getById(tenantId)
   150→		return configRepository.findByTenantId(tenant.id)
   151→	}
   152→}

   160→	)
